title: NIST AI RMF - Govern Function
content: The Govern function focuses on organizational policies, processes, roles, responsibilities, and practices that support AI risk management across the AI lifecycle.

title: NIST AI RMF - Map Function
content: The Map function focuses on understanding the context, intended purpose, stakeholders, impacts, data, and risk profile of an AI system before and during deployment.

title: NIST AI RMF - Measure Function
content: The Measure function focuses on evaluating, testing, analyzing, benchmarking, and monitoring AI risks, system behavior, and performance characteristics.

title: NIST AI RMF - Manage Function
content: The Manage function focuses on prioritizing, responding to, mitigating, and monitoring AI risks based on risk assessments, organizational tolerance, and system context.

title: AI Governance Checklist - Human Review
content: AI systems that affect employees, customers, access to services, benefits, security decisions, legal rights, or high-impact business decisions should include clear human review, escalation, override, and accountability procedures.

title: AI Governance Checklist - Logging and Traceability
content: AI systems should log enough information to support reviewability and accountability, including timestamps, system version, model version, input category, output category, user role, decision status, and human approval where applicable.

title: AI Governance Checklist - Data Minimization
content: AI systems should limit data collection, retention, and processing to what is necessary for the stated purpose. Sensitive or personal data should have access controls, retention limits, and documented handling requirements.

title: AI Governance Checklist - High-Impact Use
content: AI use cases involving employment, education, finance, healthcare, legal rights, benefits, security decisions, or access to important services should be treated as higher-risk and should require stronger review, testing, monitoring, and documentation.

title: GRC Checklist - Risk Register Entry
content: A useful risk register entry should include a risk title, risk description, affected system or process, likelihood, impact, risk level, existing controls, control gaps, recommended controls, risk owner, evidence needed, and next steps.

title: GRC Checklist - Evidence Request
content: A useful evidence request should identify the control or risk being tested, the system or process owner, the required date range, acceptable evidence examples, expected format, and follow-up questions if evidence is incomplete.
